title: Granta Omega
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Citation}} {{Character |type=Criminal |image=[[File:Granta_Omega.jpg]] |name=Granta Omega |homeworld=[[Nierport VII]] |birth=[[50 BBY/Legends|50 BBY]], [[Telos IV]] |death=[[23 BBY/Legends|23 BBY]], [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |feathers= |eyes=Blue |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents=*[[Tura Omega]]''[[Jedi Quest: The Shadow Trap]]'' *[[Xanatos]] |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Order of the Sith Lords]] *[[Offworld Mining Corporation]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I have, let's see - one, two, four, eight Jedi, all sent to capture little old me.|Granta Omega to [[Jenna Zan Arbor]]|''[[Jedi Quest: The Final Showdown]]''}} '''Granta Omega''' was a [[Human/Legends|human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] criminal and the son of the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Xanatos]] and the grandson of [[Telos IV|Telosian]] [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Crion]]. Omega vowed revenge on the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] for his father's [[Death/Legends|death]], not wanting to accept the fact that Xanatos killed himself. ==Biography== ===Early life=== Granta Omega was born on [[Telos IV]] eighteen [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]] to [[Tura Omega]] and the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Xanatos]], who became the First Citizen of [[Telos IV]]. At age three, he was sent with his mother to hide on the moon of [[Nierport VII]], where Tura worked at a refueling station. Xanatos told his son everything about the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], his former [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]], and how much they "misunderstood" [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. While he hoped that Omega would be [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], he soon realized this was not the case. Nevertheless, upon his death, he left his fortune from the [[Offworld Mining Corporation]] to Omega. As a young man, Omega left his home to attend the [[All Science Research Academy]] on [[Yerphonia]] sponsored by [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Sano Sauro]]. His mother died two years later; he never came back to Nierport VII for the funeral. ===Harassing the Jedi=== {{Quote|You Jedi. Always so resolute ... All that self-righteousness gives me the spooks.|Granta Omega, to 16-year-old Jedi Padawan Anakin Skywalker|}} [[File:AnakinObiWanOmega-NEC.jpg|thumb|left|Kenobi and Skywalker encountering Omega.]] A few years following the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], Omega hired a number of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] to capture the Jedi training on [[Ragoon VI]]. The Jedi that were there, [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and his apprentice [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] (accompanied by their Jedi pilot [[Wren Honoran]]), managed to evade the numerous traps set out by the bounty hunters, including a trap that Omega himself set. Omega reappeared in [[26 BBY/Legends|26 BBY]] under the alias of "[[Doctor/Legends|Dr.]] Tic Verdun" to attack Kenobi and Skywalker while on a [[Mission to Haariden|mission to rescue]] five war-besieged scientists on [[Haariden]]. There, Obi-Wan discovered that Omega planned to dominate the galactic [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] market through the mining of [[titanite]] on Haariden. Returning to [[Galactic City/Legends|Galactic City]], Kenobi delved into research on Omega and discovered, with the help of Jedi Temple archivist [[Jocasta Nu/Legends|Jocasta Nu]], that he was a native of [[Nierport VII|Nierport Seven]]—a small [[Moon/Legends|moon]] less than a day's journey from Coruscant. With a penchant for collecting [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] artifacts, but possessing no [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force sensitivity]] at all, Omega was extremely wealthy, though Kenobi could not determine the vast sources of his wealth. After Omega later captured Anakin in an attempt to impress the galaxy's [[Palpatine/Legends|hidden Sith Lord]], the boy managed to escape and, along with his Master, stopped Omega from pursuing his titanite venture by literally chasing him off planet when Haariden's [[Kaachtari|great volcano]] erupted. While the Jedi were caught in the disaster, they managed to blast themselves free and catch up with Omega in a final confrontation, pinning him between themselves and a great tidal wave. Even so, the ever-elusive Omega again escaped Kenobi and Skywalker's grasp, eluding capture to slip offworld. ===Revelation=== {{Quote|Choose to leave you may not, but warn you I must. Hunted you will be, by Senate security forces. By midday, under our control Naatan will be."
"Impressed with your speed, I am."
"And wish he did for me to tell you, Obi-Wan will meet you soon."
"I'm sure he did. I wish I could say I'm looking forward to it, but Kenobi puts me to sleep.|Granta "Striker" Omega and Jedi Master Yaddle|}} In [[25 BBY/Legends|25 BBY]], Omega appeared on the world of [[Mawan/Legends|Mawan]] disguised as a crime-lord named '''Striker''' (so named because of the [[Striker projectile pistol|projectile pistols]] his gang used for their first raid on its populace). Obi-Wan Kenobi, Anakin Skywalker, and [[Yaddle/Legends|Yaddle]] were assigned to the planet to negotiate peace between the warring factions there (not knowing that it was Omega who was the orchestrator of everything going on there; indeed, he was reputed to have the most extensive weapons cache of all the crimelords). Omega once again captured Skywalker, and this time was not averse to killing him, his Jedi Masters (Kenobi, Yaddle, Yoda), and all of Mawan with a bioweapon, to further impress the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]]. Though Skywalker escaped and Mawan was protected, Omega succeeded in killing Master Yaddle, who had been protecting Anakin at the time. Omega prepared to escape Mawan from the heart of its capital city [[Naatan]], and Obi-Wan Kenobi tried to stop him. It was during his escape that Omega revealed to Kenobi that he was the son of Xanatos and grandson of [[Crion]]. Kenobi was utterly astonished by this news, but realized that it all made sense. Omega obviously inherited his father's fortune from [[Offworld Mining Corporation]], and his father's hatred of the Jedi. ===Attacking the Senate=== [[File:Granta_Omega_attacks.jpg|thumb|200px|Omega attacks [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]].]] Over the next few months, Omega worked with the scientist [[Jenna Zan Arbor]]. Together they planned to use the neuro-drug known as the [[Zone of Self-Containment]] to control thousands of innocent people. In these months, Kenobi and Skywalker encountered Zan Arbor, and were led through a string of puzzles on worlds such as [[Romin]] and [[Falleen (planet)/Legends|Falleen]] that led them to finding Omega on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Here they discovered that Omega planned to assassinate [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. Though the assassination was [[Attack on the Senate Building|foiled]], and Palpatine was protected by the Jedi Padawan Ferus Olin, a number of innocent lives were taken (including [[Senatorial aide]] [[Tyro Caladian]], who had learned of Palpatine's true identity, but was unable to inform anyone before he was killed).''[[Jedi Quest: The False Peace]]'' After a battle with Obi-Wan on his [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]], Omega managed to escape the Jedi yet again. ===Omega's last stand=== Omega was finally granted an audience with the Sith on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] and took Zan Arbor with him. He met with the Sith Lord in a tomb in the [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]], and moments later, encountered the Jedi. He shot and killed [[Darra Thel-Tanis]] with his [[KYD-21 blaster pistol/Legends|KYD-21 blaster pistol]] when she tried to protect fellow Padawans [[Ferus Olin]] and [[Tru Veld/Legends|Tru Veld]]. After that, he fled and was followed by Obi-Wan. Omega manned an [[E-Web heavy repeating blaster/Legends|E-Web heavy repeating blaster]] against Kenobi. His weapon overheated and exploded. Omega was thrown against a wall. He made another attempt to attack Kenobi, but was too weak, and Obi-Wan was forced to kill Omega with his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. Omega's last words were "I know who he is. You will wish you did," referring to Darth Sidious. ===Legacy=== [[File:GrantaOmega.jpg|thumb|Granta Omega]] A few months after the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Obi-Wan revealed to [[Ferus Olin]] that Omega had been meeting with Count [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], who had been known by that time as Sidious's [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]], Darth Tyranus. ==Skills and abilities== Granta Omega possessed highly developed skills of deception; more than once, he escaped detection while right under the Jedi's noses. Omega was a [[Force void/Legends|Force Blank]]—a being with no connection to the Force, who could cloak himself as it were in a "void"—essentially shrouding himself from all detection by Force-sensitives. He used this to his advantage. People forgot they had seen him, or if they did remember him, they often could not quite place the face or how he'd originally appeared to them. Omega studied and practiced a variety of languages. He could both speak, read, and write [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], [[Pak Pak/Legends|Pak Pak]], [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]], and [[Sith (language)|ancient Sith]].{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030612omega|text=Granta Omega: An Expanded Universe Character}} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Granta Omega}} *''[[Jedi Quest: The Trail of the Jedi]]'' {{Ab|Jedi Quest: The Trail of the Jedi (audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *''[[Jedi Quest: The Dangerous Games]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[Jedi Quest: The Master of Disguise]]'' *''[[Jedi Quest: The School of Fear]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Jedi Quest: The Shadow Trap]]'' *''[[Jedi Quest: The Changing of the Guard]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Jedi Quest: The False Peace]]'' *''[[Jedi Quest: The Final Showdown]]'' *''[[The Last of the Jedi: The Desperate Mission]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[The Last of the Jedi: A Tangled Web]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030612omega|text=Granta Omega: An Expanded Universe Character}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Granta Omega |es=Granta Omega |fi=Granta Omega }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Omega, Granta}} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Impersonators]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Sith minions]]
